avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will continue to slowly consolidate and stability will be good. The surface will become firm overnight, softening during the day. Remaining cornices will be prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It will remain dry with light variable winds. The freezing level will be above the summits.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a dry day with moderate South-Westerly winds. The freezing level was above the summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack continues to slowly consolidate and stability is generally good. Remaining cornices are prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will remain well bonded and stable. The snow surface will become firmer overnight and soften where exposed to the sun during the day. Cornices that remain above some North to East facing slopes will be prone to collapse where exposed to the sun. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: High pressure will bring a dry sunny day with light and variable winds. The freezing level will be above the summits however terrain may be frozen at lower levels.
Observed Weather Influences: A mild, dry day with moderate South-Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded and stable. Greatest amounts remain in high North and East facing Cories. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will remain well bonded and stable throughout. Shaded aspects will be firm, whereas slopes exposed to direct sunlight will develop a softer surface layer. Snow patches and ribbons will remain in depressions and hollows below 950 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: High pressure will continue, dry conditions throughout. In general the freezing level will be above the summits, however the airmass over the mountains will remain cooler with summit temperatures dipping to -1.5 Celsius during the early morning. Winds will be light and variable in direction overnight before strengthening through the day to become moderate to fresh South-South-Westerlies.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry sunny day with moderate Westerly winds.  The 1000 metre freezing level rose to above the summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack has consolidated and is firm well bonded and stable throughout.  Shaded aspects are particularly firm above 900 metres, slopes exposed to direct sunlight have a softer surface layer. Snow patches and ribbons remain in depressions and hollows below 950 metres.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Cooler conditions during Wednesday night will maintain the stable snowpack in all locations and elevations. It may also become a bit more firm and icy. The greatest continuous snow amounts will remain mainly on North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres with patchy cover below this level. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: High pressure dominates across Scotland.  Staying generally dry throughout. Freezing level above the summits but clear overnight skies could give some a patchy frost at lower levels. Light winds which will vary in direction strengthening towards the end of the day from a Southerly direction.
Observed Weather Influences: It was dry, clear and quite calm but it has remained mild with summit temperature (1100 metres) of plus 2.5 degrees celsius. Winds were light from a South-Westerly direction.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack has consolidated at all levels and is firm and stable. The greatest continuous snow amounts exist mainly on North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres patchy cover below this level. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will be generally well bonded firm and stable. The greatest amounts will be found on North through East to South-East aspects above 800 metres. Any remaining old cornices will be vulnerable to collapse during the afternoon rise in temperature. Outwith these areas and above 600 metres the snow cover is patchy and limited to burns lines and sheltered hollows. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Mainly dry throughout the forecast period. Moderate Westerly winds will become light and variable. Clear skies overnight will allow a refreeze of the snowpack at all levels. Sunny during the morning then cloudy in the afternoon when summit temperatures will rise to around 3 degrees.
Observed Weather Influences: Mostly dry with the odd shower of rain and drizzle. The freezing level dropped to around 1000 metres overnight then lifted above the summits during the day. Moderate South-West winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The remaining patchy snow pack has consolidated and is generally stable. Greatest amounts of snow are found in high coires and gully lines with a North through East to South-East facing aspect above 800 metres. The remnants of old slumping cornices are mostly stable. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will continue to consolidate in the mild temperatures and will have generally good stability throughout. Turning firm through the evening before softening during the day, especially where exposed to direct sunlight. Greatest snow amounts will be found on high plateaus and high North-West through North to East facing coires. Gradual snow loss at lower elevations. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Remaining dry and mild, with the freezing level around 1200 metres. Light variable winds becoming moderate from the South during the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry and mild day with long sunny spells. Light winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack continues to consolidate in the mild temperatures and has good stability throughout. The snowpack was firm at first slowly softening during the day with the exception of high, shady locations where it remains firmer. Greatest snow amounts can be found on high plateaus and high North-West through North to East facing coires. Gradual thaw and loss of snow at lower levels. The avalanche hazard is Low.
